kill_fact: The setup survives the kill-check on the three structural vectors: (1) CATALYST LIVE/DATED/BINDING - yes; signed plan of arrangement at C$44.00/share all-cash, shareholders approved 2026-03-31 with 99%+ in favor, hard outside date 2026-07-29 (extended once from 2026-05-29), deal neither closed nor broken as of 2026-06-29. (2) TARGET REAL - yes; C$44.00 cash is the contractual consideration, not a model. (3) FLOOR REAL - yes; a break strips the premium but not the gold ounces, no financing-out/CVR/going-concern flagged, so the ~US$23 standalone floor is structural (if thin). BUT the dossier I was attacking gets the central load-bearing fact WRONG: it frames Canada/ICA as 'the hardest approval, already done' and the remaining gate as routine 'African host-country sign-offs.' The actual gating risk is China's NDRC outbound-investment approval - which the dossier never mentions and which is ACTIVELY DELAYED over (a) whether Zijin's ~27% premium is reasonable and (b) Mali geopolitical/security risk. The May->July extension was forced by the NDRC impasse, not African paperwork. The asymmetry is real, but the one open binary is a contested sovereign-discretion approval, not the near-done formality the dossier implies - so this is CONFIRMED only after correcting which regulator is the live risk.
corrections: 1) The binding outstanding condition is China's NDRC outbound-investment approval, NOT the residual African host-country sign-offs the dossier emphasizes; NDRC is delaying over premium-reasonableness and Mali risk (Reuters/Globe & Mail, INN 2026-06-02). 2) The dossier's 'the hardest approval (Canada) is already done' is misleading - Canada cleared, but the harder Chinese approval is the open, contested gate, with a plausible further slip toward ~2026-09-29 if NDRC stalls past 2026-07-29. 3) NDRC's stated Mali concern correlates the deal-break risk with the standalone floor's geopolitical exposure - a break and a weak floor can arrive together, so treat the ~US$23 floor as soft. 4) USD target floats with USD/CAD (~US$31.5 is FX-sensitive, not fixed); refresh live price and FX at sizing.
evidence: Allied Gold press release (GlobeNewswire) + SEC 6-K, 2026-05-29: ICA approval received completing Canada; ECOWAS and COMESA merger clearances obtained; African host-country approvals 'obtained or in advanced stages'; Outside Date extended to July 29, 2026; 'Any further extension... will require the parties' mutual agreement' - https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/29/3303792/0/en/Allied-Gold-Advances-Receipt-of-Regulatory-Approvals-and-Clearances-in-Connection-with-the-Proposed-Arrangement-with-Zijin-Gold-International-and-Extends-the-Outside-Date-to-July-2.html

Investing News Network, 2026-06-02: China's NDRC is delaying Zijin's US$4B Allied Gold takeover; concerns are the ~27% premium over the undisturbed 30-day average and Mali political/security risk (~half of Allied's output); reason for the May->July extension - https://investingnews.com/zijin-gold-allied-takeover-delayed/

StockTitan summary of Allied Gold 6-K, 2026-05-29: shareholders approved the arrangement 2026-03-31 with over 99% in favor; completion remains subject to outstanding regulatory approvals; no Chinese (NDRC/MOFCOM/SAFE) clearance disclosed as obtained - https://www.stocktitan.net/news/AAUC/allied-gold-advances-receipt-of-regulatory-approvals-and-clearances-ghyudt95dwpr.html

Allied Gold Corp Form 6-K, SEC EDGAR CIK 0001993344 (FY2026): definitive arrangement with Zijin Gold International at C$44.00/share cash (~C$5.5B), and regulatory-approval / outside-date progress - https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001993344/000117184326003808/exh_991.htm
